title: json_serialize_sql has 4 named arguments, but there is no 4-arg version is registered.
description:
### What happens?

The function json_serialize_sql() has 4 named arguments. 
3 of them are currently documented, the 4th one has a pending doc issue: https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb-web/issues/5009

We can call the function with each named argument, but when we try to pass all 4 of them, a binder error occurs indicating no 4-argument version is know.

### To Reproduce

`select json_serialize_sql('select 1 union all select 1', skip_default:=true,skip_empty:=true,skip_null:=true,format:=true);`

result:
```
Binder Error:
No function matches the given name and argument types 'json_serialize_sql(STRING_LITERAL, BOOLEAN, BOOLEAN, BOOLEAN, BOOLEAN)'. You might need to add explicit type casts.
        Candidate functions:
        json_serialize_sql(VARCHAR) -> JSON
        json_serialize_sql(VARCHAR, BOOLEAN) -> JSON
        json_serialize_sql(VARCHAR, BOOLEAN, BOOLEAN) -> JSON
        json_serialize_sql(VARCHAR, BOOLEAN, BOOLEAN, BOOLEAN) -> JSON
```

### OS:

Windows 11 Enterprise

### DuckDB Version:

1.2.1 (ga), v1.3.0-dev1112 981c7a8573 nightly

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Roland Bouman

### Affiliation:

EPAM Systems BV

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a nightly build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_json (id INT, data JSON, is_active BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_json (id, data, is_active) VALUES (1, JSON_OBJECT('key', 'value'), TRUE);
SELECT JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_OBJECT('data', data)) FROM test_json WHERE is_active = TRUE;
SELECT JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_OBJECT('data', data)) FROM test_json WHERE id = 1;
SELECT JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_OBJECT('data', data)) FROM test_json WHERE created_at < CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;
key_bug_features: Calling json_serialize_sql with four named arguments
